summary:
# Summary

The provided source text is not an empirical research article but rather a descriptive resource and session summary associated with a live-streamed tabletop role-playing game (TTRPG) event. The central subject is *Halo ‘n’ Horns*, a comedy micro RPG created by Connie Chang. The text explicitly defines the game’s core mechanic: it is designed for two Game Masters, who assume the roles of an angel and a devil, and one player, who portrays a mortal that the GMs are attempting to "win over." This structure suggests a competitive yet collaborative narrative framework focused on persuasion and character interaction within a supernatural context.

The document serves multiple informational functions relevant to Games-Based Library Services (GBLS). First, it provides bibliographic and contextual metadata, identifying the creator and offering links to social media profiles. Second, it situates *Halo ‘n’ Horns* within a broader ecosystem of similar games and media. The "Play Alikes" section lists other TTRPGs with thematic or mechanical parallels, such as *Heaven Has No Taste*, *Heaven Nor Hell*, and *Blazing Hymn*. The "Read/Watch Alikes" section connects the game to popular culture touchstones including *Good Omens*, *The Devil is a Part-Timer*, *Lucky Penny*, *Sweet Paprika*, *Heaven’s Design Team*, *The Good Place*, *The City We Became*, and *American Gods*. These comparisons help librarians or facilitators understand the tone, genre, and audience appeal of the game, suggesting it aligns with comedic, supernatural, and morally ambiguous narratives.

A significant portion of the text is dedicated to safety and moderation protocols, which are critical considerations for library-based gaming programs. The document lists specific "Safety Tools" recommended for use during play, including "Script Change" by Beau Jágr Sheldon and "The Door is Open" by Eirik Fatland, along with references to the TTRPG Safety Toolkit. These tools are designed to help participants manage content boundaries and ensure a comfortable environment for all players. Furthermore, the text outlines the "Game RT Twitch Policy," which establishes clear behavioral expectations for online audiences: participants must be kind and respectful, hate speech is prohibited, moderators reserve the right to remove disruptive individuals, and channels for reporting harassment or private communication are provided. These policies highlight the importance of structured moderation in public or semi-public gaming events, a key lesson for libraries hosting similar programs.

The text also summarizes the structure of the specific streaming event hosted by GameRT, an initiative associated with the American Library Association (ALA), as indicated by the contact email `gamert@ala.org`. The session was divided into three parts: an introduction and rules explanation, the actual play demonstration, and a post-game discussion. The "Actual Play" featured participant Aabria Iyengar, a well-known figure in the TTRPG community. While the text does not provide empirical data or analysis of player engagement, it offers a model for how such events can be structured: beginning with clear instruction, moving to demonstration, and concluding with reflective discussion.

In the context of a GBLS literature review, this document contributes by illustrating the practical components of facilitating a TTRPG event. It underscores the necessity of selecting appropriate games based on thematic alignment, integrating safety mechanisms to protect participants, and establishing clear codes of conduct for both players and observers. The absence of empirical findings means the text cannot inform statistical trends or measured outcomes regarding library gaming programs. However, it serves as a valuable case study in event planning and resource curation, demonstrating how libraries can leverage existing TTRPG titles while prioritizing community safety and inclusive practices. The reference to GameRT resources further points librarians toward established networks and support systems for implementing games-based services.
sourceText:
Halo ‘n’ Horns by Connie Chang
“Halo 'n' Horns is a comedy micro RPG for two GMs — an angel and a devil — and one player: the mortal they're trying to win over.”
Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/tpuBAP2vGhcF7A
The Game Halo ‘n’ Horns by Connie Chang The Creator
● Connie Chang’s Twitter ● Connie Chang’s carrd.co
Play Alikes ● Heaven Has No Taste; And Other True Statements by ATLAS, gamesfromthewildwood ● Heaven Nor Hell by kiennas ● Blazing Hymn by Peach Garden Games ● To Reign in Hell by Cody Faulk ● The Last Emissary by E. R. F. Jordan ● Relics: An Angel’s Guide to Las Vegas by Tin Star Games ● Ye Who Abandon Hope, Please Sign Here by Trystan Goetze ● The Details in the Devil by Starshine Scribbles ● And I Saw Him On The Train by Riverhouse Games
Read/Watch Alikes: ● Good Omens- Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman (Amazon Prime & BBC2) ● The Devil is a Part-Timer- Satoshi Wagahara & Akio Hiragi (Yen Press, White Fox) ● Lucky Penny- Anath Hirsch & Yuko Ota (Oni Press) ● Sweet Paprika- Mirka Andolfo (Image) ● Heaven’s Design Team- Hebi-zou, Tsuta Suzuki, Tarako (Kodansha, Asahi Production) ● The Good Place- Michael Schur (NBC)

● The City We Became- N K Jemisin ● American Gods- Neil Gaiman (Starz)
Actual Plays ● 2+U | Halo 'n' Horns w/ Aabria Iyengar
Safety Tools ● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive
Game RT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
Stream Summary Tech Check on Twitch then Go Time! Part 1: Intro and Rules Part 2: Actual Play Part 3: Discussion
